kids.question: What is the beginning of knowledge according to Proverbs 1?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 1:7 says, "The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge."

kids.question: Who wrote the book of Proverbs according to chapter 1?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 1:1 says, "The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 1 say a child should listen to?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 1:8 says, "Hear, the instruction of thy father."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 1:7 say fools despise?
teens.option_a: Wisdom and instruction
teens.option_b: Their neighbours
teens.option_c: Hard work
teens.option_d: The king
teens.explanation: Proverbs 1:7 says, "but fools despise wisdom and instruction."

teens.question: In Proverbs 1, what does wisdom do in the streets?
teens.option_a: She uttereth her voice
teens.option_b: She weeps and mourns
teens.option_c: She builds a house
teens.option_d: She plants a garden
teens.explanation: Proverbs 1:20 says, "Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 1 warn against joining?
teens.option_a: Sinners who lay in wait for blood
teens.option_b: Foreign armies
teens.option_c: Strange religions
teens.option_d: Merchants on the road
teens.explanation: Proverbs 1:10-11 warns, "if sinners entice thee...they lay wait for blood."

teens.question: What happens to those who reject wisdom's call according to Proverbs 1?
teens.option_a: They will prosper in their own way
teens.option_b: They will eat the fruit of their own way
teens.option_c: They will find another teacher
teens.option_d: They will be given a second chance
teens.explanation: Proverbs 1:31 says, "Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way."

teens.question: What will the LORD pour out when people seek Him after rejecting wisdom?
teens.option_a: His Spirit
teens.option_b: His anger
teens.option_c: His judgement
teens.option_d: His silence
teens.explanation: Proverbs 1:23 says, "I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you."

adults.question: What is the stated purpose of the proverbs in Proverbs 1:2-4?
adults.option_a: To know wisdom and instruction, to perceive words of understanding
adults.option_b: To entertain the people of Israel
adults.option_c: To record the history of Solomon
adults.option_d: To teach the law of Moses
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:2 states, "To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding."

adults.question: What instruction regarding ornamental grace does Proverbs 1:9 give?
adults.option_a: The instruction of parents shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head
adults.option_b: Wisdom is a crown for the head
adults.option_c: A soft answer turns away wrath
adults.option_d: The fear of the LORD adds years to life
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:9 says, "For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck."

adults.question: How does Wisdom react when calamity falls on those who rejected her according to Proverbs 1:26?
adults.option_a: She will laugh at their calamity and mock when their fear cometh
adults.option_b: She mourns with them
adults.option_c: She offers them another chance
adults.option_d: She intercedes for them
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:26 says, "I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh."

adults.question: What three things does Proverbs 1:4 say the proverbs give to the simple?
adults.option_a: Subtilty, and to the young man knowledge and discretion
adults.option_b: Wealth, honour, and long life
adults.option_c: Peace, joy, and understanding
adults.option_d: Faith, hope, and love
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:4 says, "To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion."

adults.question: In Proverbs 1:17, what illustration does Solomon use about setting a trap?
adults.option_a: A bird is not caught if it sees the net spread
adults.option_b: A fish sees the bait and avoids the hook
adults.option_c: A deer sees the snare and leaps away
adults.option_d: A lion sees the hunter and hides
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:17 says, "Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 1:33 promise to the one who hearkens to wisdom?
adults.option_a: He shall dwell safely and be quiet from fear of evil
adults.option_b: He shall have silver and gold
adults.option_c: He shall rule over nations
adults.option_d: He shall live a thousand years
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:33 says, "But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 1:6 indicate these proverbs will help a wise man understand?
adults.option_a: A proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings
adults.option_b: The laws of the king
adults.option_c: The paths of the righteous
adults.option_d: The prophecies of the prophets
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:6 says, "To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings."

adults.question: In Proverbs 1:28-29, why will wisdom not answer when sinners call upon her?
adults.option_a: Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD
adults.option_b: Because she is busy with the righteous
adults.option_c: Because God has sealed her lips
adults.option_d: Because the time for mercy is past forever
adults.explanation: Proverbs 1:29 says, "For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the LORD."

kids.question: Where does wisdom come from according to Proverbs 2?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 2:6 says, "For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 2 say will protect you like a shield?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 2:7 says God is "a buckler to them that walk uprightly."

kids.question: What kind of paths does wisdom help you understand according to Proverbs 2?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 2:9 says wisdom will help you understand "every good path."

teens.question: What must a person do to find knowledge of God according to Proverbs 2:3-4?
teens.option_a: Cry after wisdom and search for her as silver
teens.option_b: Offer many sacrifices
teens.option_c: Travel to Jerusalem
teens.option_d: Ask wise elders
teens.explanation: Proverbs 2:3-4 says to "cry after knowledge...and searchest for her as for hid treasures."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 2 say wisdom will deliver you from?
teens.option_a: The way of the evil man and the strange woman
teens.option_b: Poverty and sickness
teens.option_c: Enemies in battle
teens.option_d: Hunger and thirst
teens.explanation: Proverbs 2:12 says wisdom will deliver you "from the way of the evil man."

teens.question: How is the strange woman's house described in Proverbs 2:18?
teens.option_a: Her house inclineth unto death
teens.option_b: Her house is full of riches
teens.option_c: Her house is built on a rock
teens.option_d: Her house stands near the temple
teens.explanation: Proverbs 2:18 says, "For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 2:20 say the wise person will do?
teens.option_a: Walk in the way of good men
teens.option_b: Avoid all strangers
teens.option_c: Seek silver and gold
teens.option_d: Dwell in the mountains
teens.explanation: Proverbs 2:20 says, "That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous."

teens.question: What will the upright dwell in according to Proverbs 2:21?
teens.option_a: The land
teens.option_b: The king's palace
teens.option_c: The temple courts
teens.option_d: The mountains
teens.explanation: Proverbs 2:21 says, "For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it."

adults.question: What two qualities does Proverbs 2:7 say God lays up for the righteous?
adults.option_a: Sound wisdom and a buckler
adults.option_b: Silver and gold
adults.option_c: Long life and peace
adults.option_d: Honour and strength
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:7 says, "He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walk uprightly."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 2:5 say seeking wisdom leads to?
adults.option_a: Understanding the fear of the LORD and finding the knowledge of God
adults.option_b: Wealth and prosperity
adults.option_c: Fame among the nations
adults.option_d: Victory over enemies
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:5 says, "Then shalt thou understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 2:16-17 describe the strange woman's spiritual condition?
adults.option_a: She forsaketh the guide of her youth and forgetteth the covenant of her God
adults.option_b: She never knew the LORD
adults.option_c: She worships idols openly
adults.option_d: She is a stranger from another nation
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:17 says she "forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of her God."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 2:11 say will preserve and keep the seeker of wisdom?
adults.option_a: Discretion and understanding
adults.option_b: Prayer and fasting
adults.option_c: Parents and teachers
adults.option_d: Laws and statutes
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:11 says, "Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee."

adults.question: What is the fate of the wicked in Proverbs 2:22?
adults.option_a: The wicked shall be cut off from the earth
adults.option_b: The wicked shall wander in the wilderness
adults.option_c: The wicked shall lose their wealth
adults.option_d: The wicked shall be enslaved
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:22 says, "But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 2:8 say God preserveth?
adults.option_a: The paths of judgment and the way of his saints
adults.option_b: The words of the prophets
adults.option_c: The boundaries of Israel
adults.option_d: The records of the righteous
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:8 says, "He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints."

adults.question: What condition is stated in Proverbs 2:1-2 for the benefits of wisdom to flow?
adults.option_a: If thou wilt receive words and hide commandments, inclining thine ear unto wisdom
adults.option_b: If thou offerest sacrifices daily
adults.option_c: If thou keepest the Sabbath
adults.option_d: If thou teachest others
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:1-2 says, "If thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee; so that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 2:10 say will happen when wisdom enters the heart?
adults.option_a: Knowledge will be pleasant unto thy soul
adults.option_b: Riches will follow thee
adults.option_c: The LORD will speak to thee
adults.option_d: Enemies will flee from thee
adults.explanation: Proverbs 2:10 says, "When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 3:5 say we should trust with all our heart?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 3:5 says, "Trust in the LORD with all thine heart."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 3:6 say will happen if we acknowledge God in all our ways?
kids.option_d: He shall give us long life
kids.explanation: Proverbs 3:6 says, "In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths."

kids.question: What should we honour the LORD with according to Proverbs 3?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 3:9 says, "Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 3:5 warn us NOT to lean on?
teens.option_a: Our own understanding
teens.option_b: The strength of others
teens.option_c: The wisdom of the world
teens.option_d: The riches of men
teens.explanation: Proverbs 3:5 says, "lean not unto thine own understanding."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 3:11-12 say about the LORD's chastening?
teens.option_a: Do not despise it, for the LORD corrects those He loves
teens.option_b: Pray that it ends quickly
teens.option_c: Only the wicked are chastened
teens.option_d: It means God has forgotten you
teens.explanation: Proverbs 3:11-12 says, "despise not the chastening of the LORD...for whom the LORD loveth he correcteth."

teens.question: What is wisdom's right hand said to hold in Proverbs 3:16?
teens.option_a: Riches and honour
teens.option_b: Length of days
teens.option_c: Understanding
teens.option_d: Peace and safety
teens.explanation: Proverbs 3:16 says, "Length of days is in her right hand; and in her left hand riches and honour."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 3:27 instruct about doing good?
teens.option_a: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due
teens.option_b: Do good only to your family
teens.option_c: Wait for the right moment
teens.option_d: Do good secretly
teens.explanation: Proverbs 3:27 says, "Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 3:34 say God does to the scorners but gives to the lowly?
teens.option_a: He scorneth the scorners but giveth grace unto the lowly
teens.option_b: He ignores the scorners but blesses the lowly with wealth
teens.option_c: He punishes the scorners but saves the lowly
teens.option_d: He silences the scorners but speaks to the lowly
teens.explanation: Proverbs 3:34 says, "Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 3:3 instruct about mercy and truth?
adults.option_a: Bind them about thy neck, write them upon the table of thine heart
adults.option_b: Teach them to thy children daily
adults.option_c: Seek them in the house of the LORD
adults.option_d: Store them in thy heart and mind
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:3 says, "Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 3:7 warn against?
adults.option_a: Being wise in thine own eyes; fear the LORD and depart from evil
adults.option_b: Trusting in wealth
adults.option_c: Following strange gods
adults.option_d: Despising the poor
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:7 says, "Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil."

adults.question: What is the reward promised in Proverbs 3:10 for honouring God with firstfruits?
adults.option_a: Barns filled with plenty and vats overflowing with new wine
adults.option_b: A long life and peaceful death
adults.option_c: Victory over enemies
adults.option_d: Favour with the king
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:10 says, "So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine."

adults.question: How is wisdom described in Proverbs 3:17-18?
adults.option_a: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace; she is a tree of life
adults.option_b: She is a lamp unto the feet
adults.option_c: She is more precious than rubies alone
adults.option_d: She stands at the city gate
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:17-18 says, "Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace. She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 3:19-20 say wisdom was used for?
adults.option_a: The LORD founded the earth by wisdom and established the heavens by understanding
adults.option_b: Writing the law of Moses
adults.option_c: Building the temple
adults.option_d: Dividing the Red Sea
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:19 says, "The LORD by wisdom hath founded the earth; by understanding hath he established the heavens."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 3:30 warn against?
adults.option_a: Striving with a man without cause when he hath done thee no harm
adults.option_b: Making promises you cannot keep
adults.option_c: Lending money to the poor
adults.option_d: Boasting of tomorrow
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:30 says, "Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm."

adults.question: What blessings does Proverbs 3:24 promise to the one who keeps wisdom?
adults.option_a: When thou liest down thou shalt not be afraid; thy sleep shall be sweet
adults.option_b: Wealth and long life
adults.option_c: Victory and honour
adults.option_d: Health and strength
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:24 says, "When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 3:13 declare about the man who finds wisdom?
adults.option_a: Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding
adults.option_b: Blessed is the king who seeks counsel
adults.option_c: Rich is the man who fears the LORD
adults.option_d: Wise is the man who reads the law
adults.explanation: Proverbs 3:13 says, "Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 4 say is the principal thing we should get?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 4:7 says, "Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom."

kids.question: What does the path of the just shine like in Proverbs 4?
kids.option_a: The shining light that shineth more and more unto the perfect day
kids.explanation: Proverbs 4:18 says, "But the path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 4:23 tell us to keep above all things?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 4:23 says, "Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life."

teens.question: How did Solomon's father teach him about wisdom according to Proverbs 4?
teens.option_a: He instructed him and said, "Get wisdom, get understanding"
teens.option_b: He sent him to school
teens.option_c: He gave him books to read
teens.option_d: He showed him by example only
teens.explanation: Proverbs 4:4-5 says his father instructed, "Get wisdom, get understanding: forget it not."

teens.question: What will wisdom do for you if you embrace her according to Proverbs 4:8?
teens.option_a: She shall promote thee and bring thee to honour
teens.option_b: She shall give thee silver
teens.option_c: She shall protect thee in battle
teens.option_d: She shall make thee a king
teens.explanation: Proverbs 4:8 says, "Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 4:14-15 say about the path of the wicked?
teens.option_a: Enter not into it, avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it
teens.option_b: Walk carefully through it
teens.option_c: Only visit it to warn others
teens.option_d: Study it to understand evil
teens.explanation: Proverbs 4:14-15 says, "Enter not into the path of the wicked...Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 4:19 say about the way of the wicked?
teens.option_a: It is as darkness, they know not at what they stumble
teens.option_b: It leads to poverty and shame
teens.option_c: It ends in sudden destruction
teens.option_d: It is wide and easy to travel
teens.explanation: Proverbs 4:19 says, "The way of the wicked is as darkness: they know not at what they stumble."

teens.question: What four body parts does Proverbs 4:24-27 give instructions about?
teens.option_a: Mouth, eyes, eyelids, and feet
teens.option_b: Hands, feet, ears, and eyes
teens.option_c: Heart, mind, tongue, and hands
teens.option_d: Lips, ears, hands, and knees
teens.explanation: Proverbs 4:24-27 addresses the mouth (4:24), eyes (4:25), eyelids (4:25), and feet (4:26-27).

adults.question: What does Proverbs 4:7 say about getting wisdom even at cost?
adults.option_a: With all thy getting get understanding
adults.option_b: Sell all thou hast to buy it
adults.option_c: Seek it above silver and gold
adults.option_d: Pay any price for instruction
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:7 says, "Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding."

adults.question: What will wisdom place on your head according to Proverbs 4:9?
adults.option_a: An ornament of grace and a crown of glory
adults.option_b: A garland of flowers
adults.option_c: The diadem of kings
adults.option_d: A crown of righteousness
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:9 says, "She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 4:10 promise to the one who receives wisdom's sayings?
adults.option_a: The years of thy life shall be many
adults.option_b: Thy barns shall be filled
adults.option_c: Thy enemies shall flee
adults.option_d: Thy name shall be remembered
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:10 says, "Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 4:16-17 describe the wicked man's need to do evil?
adults.option_a: They sleep not except they have done mischief; they eat the bread of wickedness
adults.option_b: They wander seeking someone to harm
adults.option_c: They plot evil by day and execute it by night
adults.option_d: They love violence more than peace
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:16-17 says, "For they sleep not, except they have done mischief...For they eat the bread of wickedness."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 4:20-22 say about the father's words?
adults.option_a: They are life unto those that find them and health to all their flesh
adults.option_b: They are sweeter than honey
adults.option_c: They are sharper than a two-edged sword
adults.option_d: They are more precious than rubies
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:22 says, "For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh."

adults.question: What does "out of it are the issues of life" mean in Proverbs 4:23?
adults.option_a: The heart is the source of one's entire life direction and character
adults.option_b: The heart pumps blood to keep one alive
adults.option_c: Emotions come from the heart
adults.option_d: Life's problems come from a troubled heart
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:23 says "keep thy heart...for out of it are the issues of life" — the heart determines life's course.

adults.question: What specific speech instruction is given in Proverbs 4:24?
adults.option_a: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee
adults.option_b: Speak softly to your enemies
adults.option_c: Say only what is true
adults.option_d: Let your words be few
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:24 says, "Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 4:26 instruct about ordering one's ways?
adults.option_a: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established
adults.option_b: Walk only on known paths
adults.option_c: Follow the way of the righteous man
adults.option_d: Let your steps be guided by prayer
adults.explanation: Proverbs 4:26 says, "Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 5 tell a young man to rejoice with?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 5:18 says, "rejoice with the wife of thy youth."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 5 warn that a strange woman's lips drop like?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 5:3 says, "For the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 5:21 say the LORD does?
kids.option_a: His ways are before the eyes of the LORD
kids.option_d: He guides the feet of the just
kids.explanation: Proverbs 5:21 says, "For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD."

teens.question: What is the end of the strange woman's lips and feet according to Proverbs 5:3-5?
teens.option_a: Bitter as wormwood, her feet go down to death
teens.option_b: Sweet but costly
teens.option_c: Pleasing but fleeting
teens.option_d: Lovely but deadly slow
teens.explanation: Proverbs 5:4-5 says, "But her end is bitter as wormwood...Her feet go down to death."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 5:8 counsel concerning the strange woman?
teens.option_a: Remove thy way far from her and come not nigh the door of her house
teens.option_b: Warn her of her sin
teens.option_c: Pray for her repentance
teens.option_d: Report her to the elders
teens.explanation: Proverbs 5:8 says, "Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house."

teens.question: What regret does the foolish man express in Proverbs 5:12-13?
teens.option_a: How have I hated instruction and my heart despised reproof
teens.option_b: Why did I not listen to my father
teens.option_c: I have wasted all my silver
teens.option_d: I have dishonoured my family
teens.explanation: Proverbs 5:12-13 says, "How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof; and have not obeyed the voice of my teachers."

teens.question: How does Proverbs 5:15-16 use water imagery to teach faithfulness in marriage?
teens.option_a: Drink waters from thine own cistern, not let thy fountains be dispersed abroad
teens.option_b: Build your house on the rock not the sand
teens.option_c: A flowing stream brings life to the desert
teens.option_d: Still waters reflect what is in the heart
teens.explanation: Proverbs 5:15-16 says, "Drink waters out of thine own cistern...Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad."

teens.question: What fate awaits the wicked man who follows the strange woman according to Proverbs 5:22?
teens.option_a: His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself
teens.option_b: He shall lose his inheritance
teens.option_c: He shall be driven from the city
teens.option_d: He shall be put to shame before all
teens.explanation: Proverbs 5:22 says, "His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 5:6 say about the strange woman's way of life?
adults.option_a: Her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them
adults.option_b: She walks the path of destruction openly
adults.option_c: She plans every step to ensnare
adults.option_d: She pretends righteousness to deceive
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:6 says, "Her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them."

adults.question: What shame does Proverbs 5:9-10 warn will come from following the strange woman?
adults.option_a: Thine honour given unto others and thy years unto the cruel
adults.option_b: Loss of position and wealth
adults.option_c: Exile from the nation
adults.option_d: Sickness and poverty
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:9-10 says, "Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel...and thy labours be in the house of a stranger."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 5:11 say will happen at the end of a life of immorality?
adults.option_a: Thou mournest at the last when thy flesh and body are consumed
adults.option_b: You will seek wisdom but not find it
adults.option_c: Your children will be ashamed
adults.option_d: You will lose all your friends
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:11 says, "And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 5:18-19 describe the blessing of a faithful wife?
adults.option_a: As a loving hind and pleasant roe, let her breasts satisfy thee at all times
adults.option_b: As a tower of strength and comfort
adults.option_c: As a fruitful vine and gentle stream
adults.option_d: As the morning star and evening rest
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:19 says, "Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe...let her breasts satisfy thee at all times."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 5:20 use as a rhetorical question to urge faithfulness?
adults.option_a: Why wilt thou be ravished with a strange woman and embrace the bosom of a stranger
adults.option_b: Why wouldst thou leave the path of righteousness
adults.option_c: Why dost thou despise the wife of thy youth
adults.option_d: Why art thou drawn to vanity and emptiness
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:20 says, "And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?"

adults.question: What does Proverbs 5:22 teach about the self-defeating nature of sin?
adults.option_a: The cords of a man's sins hold him fast; he is holden with the cords of his sins
adults.option_b: Sin eventually leads to open shame
adults.option_c: The wicked will be punished by God alone
adults.option_d: Evil deeds come back as a snare
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:22 says, "his own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins."

adults.question: What does the image of "cistern" and "well" in Proverbs 5:15 symbolise?
adults.option_a: One's own wife as the lawful source of marital intimacy
adults.option_b: The word of God as a source of life
adults.option_c: The temple as the dwelling of the LORD
adults.option_d: A city's supply of fresh water
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:15 says, "Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well" — referring to one's own wife.

adults.question: What is the concluding fate of the man who ignores instruction according to Proverbs 5:23?
adults.option_a: He shall die without instruction and go astray through his great folly
adults.option_b: He shall be cut off from his people
adults.option_c: He shall wander in the wilderness
adults.option_d: He shall be given over to his enemies
adults.explanation: Proverbs 5:23 says, "He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray."

kids.question: What small creature does Proverbs 6 tell the lazy person to learn from?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 6:6 says, "Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise."

kids.question: How many things does the LORD hate according to Proverbs 6?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 6:16 says, "These six things doth the LORD hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 6 say a naughty person does with their fingers?
kids.option_b: He points and winks with mischief
kids.option_c: He hides what he has stolen
kids.explanation: Proverbs 6:13 says, "He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 6:1-3 warn about becoming surety (co-signing a pledge) for another?
teens.option_a: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth; deliver thyself
teens.option_b: It is a noble act of friendship
teens.option_c: It brings blessing if the friend is righteous
teens.option_d: The LORD will reward thee for it
teens.explanation: Proverbs 6:2-3 says, "Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth...deliver thyself."

teens.question: What does the ant do without having a guide, overseer, or ruler according to Proverbs 6:7-8?
teens.option_a: Provideth her meat in the summer and gathereth food in the harvest
teens.option_b: Builds her house and protects her young
teens.option_c: Follows the queen in all things
teens.option_d: Seeks wisdom from older ants
teens.explanation: Proverbs 6:7-8 says the ant, "provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest."

teens.question: What first item in the seven abominations does God hate in Proverbs 6:17?
teens.option_a: A proud look
teens.option_b: A lying tongue
teens.option_c: Hands that shed blood
teens.option_d: A froward heart
teens.explanation: Proverbs 6:17 lists first "A proud look" among the seven things the LORD hates.

teens.question: What does Proverbs 6:20-22 say about a father's commandments when you walk and when you sleep?
teens.option_a: They shall lead thee, keep thee, and talk with thee
teens.option_b: They shall be a lamp to your path
teens.option_c: They shall comfort thee in trouble
teens.option_d: They shall make thee wise among thy peers
teens.explanation: Proverbs 6:22 says, "When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 6:26 warn about the cost of following an immoral woman?
teens.option_a: By means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread
teens.option_b: She will rob thee of thy silver
teens.option_c: She leads men to ruin their health
teens.option_d: She brings shame upon a man's family
teens.explanation: Proverbs 6:26 says, "For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread."

adults.question: What four descriptors does Proverbs 6:12-13 use for a naughty and wicked person?
adults.option_a: Froward mouth, winking eyes, speaking feet, teaching fingers
adults.option_b: Proud heart, lying lips, swift feet, false witness
adults.option_c: Haughty eyes, lying tongue, wicked heart, discord sower
adults.option_d: Idle hands, wandering eyes, crooked path, false scales
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:12-13 describes: "A naughty person...walketh with a froward mouth. He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 6:15 say will happen suddenly to the wicked person who sows discord?
adults.option_a: He shall be broken without remedy
adults.option_b: He shall fall into poverty
adults.option_c: He shall be cast out of the city
adults.option_d: He shall be put to shame
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:15 says, "Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy."

adults.question: What is the seventh abomination listed in Proverbs 6:19?
adults.option_a: He that soweth discord among brethren
adults.option_b: A false witness that speaketh lies
adults.option_c: Feet that be swift in running to mischief
adults.option_d: A heart that deviseth wicked imaginations
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:19 says the seventh abomination is "he that soweth discord among brethren."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 6:23 describe the commandment and the law of a godly father?
adults.option_a: The commandment is a lamp and the law is light, and reproofs of instruction are the way of life
adults.option_b: They are sweeter than honey and more precious than gold
adults.option_c: They are the foundation of wisdom and the path of peace
adults.option_d: They are arrows in the hand of a warrior
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:23 says, "For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 6:30-31 say distinguishes theft out of hunger from adultery?
adults.option_a: A thief may be forgiven when caught but the adulterer faces wounds and dishonour
adults.option_b: Theft is punishable by law but adultery is a private sin
adults.option_c: Theft harms property but adultery harms no one
adults.option_d: A thief can make restitution but adultery has no remedy
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:30-32 notes a thief is not despised when hungry but the adulterer destroys his own soul and gets wounds and dishonour.

adults.question: What does Proverbs 6:34-35 say about the jealousy of a wronged husband?
adults.option_a: His jealousy will not spare in the day of vengeance; he will not accept any ransom
adults.option_b: He will seek revenge through the elders
adults.option_c: He will demand full restitution
adults.option_d: He will challenge the man to a public confrontation
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:34-35 says, "For jealousy is the rage of a man...He will not regard any ransom."

adults.question: What does the ant's behaviour in Proverbs 6:6-8 teach about planning for the future?
adults.option_a: Diligent preparation in season prevents poverty without needing external compulsion
adults.option_b: Follow a leader who sets the example
adults.option_c: Work only when the reward is clear
adults.option_d: Community effort achieves more than individual work
adults.explanation: The ant "provideth her meat in the summer" without overseer (Proverbs 6:7-8), teaching self-motivated diligence.

adults.question: What does Proverbs 6:9-11 warn will overtake the sluggard?
adults.option_a: Poverty shall come as one that travelleth and want as an armed man
adults.option_b: Shame before his neighbours
adults.option_c: Hunger and sickness
adults.option_d: Loss of his inheritance
adults.explanation: Proverbs 6:11 says, "So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 7 say to call wisdom?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 7:4 says, "Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister."

kids.question: What animal is the foolish young man compared to in Proverbs 7?
kids.option_c: A dog returning to his vomit
kids.option_d: A bird caught in a snare
kids.explanation: Proverbs 7:22 says he goes "as an ox goeth to the slaughter."

kids.question: From where did Solomon observe the foolish young man in Proverbs 7?
kids.option_a: Through a window of his house
kids.option_c: From the top of a hill
kids.option_d: From a hiding place in the street
kids.explanation: Proverbs 7:6 says, "For at the window of my house I looked through my casement."

teens.question: At what time of day did the young man approach the strange woman's house according to Proverbs 7?
teens.option_a: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night
teens.option_b: At the break of dawn
teens.option_c: In the heat of the midday sun
teens.option_d: At the busy noon hour
teens.explanation: Proverbs 7:9 says, "In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night."

teens.question: How does the strange woman dress in Proverbs 7:10?
teens.option_a: With the attire of an harlot and subtil of heart
teens.option_b: In fine linen and jewels
teens.option_c: In the robes of a widow
teens.option_d: In the garments of a merchant
teens.explanation: Proverbs 7:10 says she meets him "with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart."

teens.question: What excuse does the strange woman give for her husband's absence in Proverbs 7:19-20?
teens.option_a: The goodman is gone on a long journey and will not come home till the day appointed
teens.option_b: He is away at war
teens.option_c: He is trading in a distant city
teens.option_d: He is visiting the king
teens.explanation: Proverbs 7:19-20 says, "For the goodman is not at home...he is gone a long journey."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 7:26 say about the many victims of the strange woman?
teens.option_a: She hath cast down many wounded; yea, many strong men have been slain by her
teens.option_b: She has deceived the simple only
teens.option_c: She traps only the foolish young
teens.option_d: She has caused sorrow to many families
teens.explanation: Proverbs 7:26 says, "For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her."

teens.question: Where does the strange woman's house lead according to Proverbs 7:27?
teens.option_a: Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death
teens.option_b: Her house is a path to shame
teens.option_c: Her house leads to poverty and ruin
teens.option_d: Her house brings a man to despair
teens.explanation: Proverbs 7:27 says, "Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 7:2-3 instruct about keeping the father's commandments?
adults.option_a: Keep them as the apple of thine eye, bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon thine heart
adults.option_b: Read them morning and evening
adults.option_c: Teach them to thy children
adults.option_d: Recite them before the LORD daily
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:2-3 says, "Keep my commandments, and live...bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 7:13 describe the boldness of the strange woman?
adults.option_a: She caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him
adults.option_b: She called to him from her window
adults.option_c: She sent a servant with a message
adults.option_d: She waited quietly at her door
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:13 says, "So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him."

adults.question: What religious activity does the strange woman use as a lure in Proverbs 7:14?
adults.option_a: Peace offerings were due and she had paid her vows
adults.option_b: She invited him to a feast at the temple
adults.option_c: She claimed to have a prophecy for him
adults.option_d: She said God had sent her to him
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:14 says, "I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows."

adults.question: What three comparisons does Proverbs 7:22-23 use to describe the fate of the foolish young man?
adults.option_a: An ox to slaughter, a fool to correction, a bird to a snare
adults.option_b: A fish to the net, a moth to the flame, a dog to his vomit
adults.option_c: A sheep to the fold, a child to punishment, a deer to the arrow
adults.option_d: A lion to the trap, a fly to the web, a boar to the spear
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:22-23 says he goes "as an ox goeth to the slaughter...as a fool to the correction of the stocks; as a bird hasteth to the snare."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 7:21 say the woman used to seduce the young man?
adults.option_a: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him
adults.option_b: With gifts and fine clothing
adults.option_c: With promises of wealth
adults.option_d: With tears and pleading
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:21 says, "With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him."

adults.question: What does the phrase "he knoweth not that it is for his life" in Proverbs 7:23 emphasise?
adults.option_a: The deadly spiritual blindness of the man seduced by immorality
adults.option_b: The speed at which sin overtakes a man
adults.option_c: The certainty of divine punishment
adults.option_d: The foolishness of ignoring wisdom's warnings
adults.explanation: "He knoweth not that it is for his life" (Proverbs 7:23) shows the spiritually blinded man does not see he is walking to his own destruction.

adults.question: What instruction does Proverbs 7:24-25 give after describing the seduction?
adults.option_a: Hearken unto me, let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths
adults.option_b: Flee from the city where she dwells
adults.option_c: Warn thy brothers about her
adults.option_d: Trust in the LORD for deliverance
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:24-25 says, "Hearken unto me now therefore...Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths."

adults.question: What does the strange woman's preparation described in Proverbs 7:16-17 reveal about her seduction?
adults.option_a: She has prepared her bed with fine linen and perfumed it with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon
adults.option_b: She has prepared a great feast
adults.option_c: She has adorned herself in costly garments
adults.option_d: She has cleared her house of her husband's belongings
adults.explanation: Proverbs 7:16-17 says, "I have decked my bed with coverings...I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon."

kids.question: In Proverbs 8, where does Wisdom stand to call out to people?
kids.option_a: Upon the high places, at the gates, at the entry of the city
kids.explanation: Proverbs 8:2-3 says, "She standeth in the top of high places...at the gates, at the entry of the city."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 8 say wisdom is better than?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 8:11 says, "For wisdom is better than rubies."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 8:17 say wisdom does for those who love her?
kids.option_a: She loves them that love her
kids.explanation: Proverbs 8:17 says, "I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 8:13 say the fear of the LORD involves?
teens.option_a: Hating evil, pride, arrogance, the evil way, and the froward mouth
teens.option_b: Keeping all the commandments perfectly
teens.option_c: Offering sacrifices daily at the temple
teens.option_d: Fasting and praying without ceasing
teens.explanation: Proverbs 8:13 says, "The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate."

teens.question: What two qualities does wisdom dwell with according to Proverbs 8:12?
teens.option_a: Prudence, and she finds out knowledge and discretion
teens.option_b: Righteousness and peace
teens.option_c: Strength and honour
teens.option_d: Truth and mercy
teens.explanation: Proverbs 8:12 says, "I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions."

teens.question: What does wisdom give to kings and princes according to Proverbs 8:15-16?
teens.option_a: By me kings reign and princes decree justice
teens.option_b: She gives them long reign and victory in battle
teens.option_c: She fills their treasuries with silver
teens.option_d: She causes all nations to honour them
teens.explanation: Proverbs 8:15-16 says, "By me kings reign, and princes decree justice. By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth."

teens.question: What was wisdom's role at the time of creation according to Proverbs 8:30?
teens.option_a: She was beside God as a master workman, daily his delight
teens.option_b: She watched the creation from afar
teens.option_c: She named the animals with Adam
teens.option_d: She sang praises as God created
teens.explanation: Proverbs 8:30 says, "Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 8:35-36 say about finding or missing wisdom?
teens.option_a: Whoso findeth wisdom findeth life; he that sinneth against wisdom wrongeth his own soul
teens.option_b: Wisdom rewards the faithful and punishes the wicked
teens.option_c: Finding wisdom brings riches; missing it brings poverty
teens.option_d: Wisdom welcomes the humble and rejects the proud
teens.explanation: Proverbs 8:35-36 says, "For whoso findeth me findeth life...But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 8:6-8 say about the words of wisdom?
adults.option_a: They are excellent, right, true, and nothing froward or perverse
adults.option_b: They are sweet and pleasant to the ear
adults.option_c: They are sharper than a two-edged sword
adults.option_d: They are preserved forever in the LORD's memory
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:6-8 says, "Hear...excellent things...all the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 8:10-11 say wisdom is worth compared to choice silver?
adults.option_a: Receive her instruction rather than silver; wisdom is better than rubies and all things desired not comparable
adults.option_b: Wisdom can buy more than silver can
adults.option_c: Wisdom brings silver as its reward
adults.option_d: Both wisdom and silver are necessary
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:10-11 says, "Receive my instruction, and not silver...For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it."

adults.question: In Proverbs 8:22-23, how does Wisdom describe her origin?
adults.option_a: The LORD possessed me at the beginning of his work, before the earth was
adults.option_b: I was created on the fourth day of creation
adults.option_c: I sprang forth from the heart of God at the flood
adults.option_d: I came into being when man first sought understanding
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:22-23 says, "The LORD possessed me at the beginning of his work, the first of his acts of old. I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 8:24-26 emphasise about wisdom's pre-existence?
adults.option_a: Wisdom was brought forth before the depths, hills, fields, and the dust of the world
adults.option_b: Wisdom appeared at the same time as the angels
adults.option_c: Wisdom was formed when the first man was created
adults.option_d: Wisdom preceded the law given to Moses
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:24-26 says wisdom was brought forth before "the depths...before the mountains were settled...before he had made the earth."

adults.question: What does wisdom say she hates in Proverbs 8:7?
adults.option_a: Wickedness is an abomination to my lips
adults.option_b: The ways of the violent man
adults.option_c: The counsel of the froward
adults.option_d: The proud and arrogant
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:7 says, "for my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 8:19 say wisdom's fruit is better than?
adults.option_a: Fine gold; her revenue better than choice silver
adults.option_b: Honey from the rock
adults.option_c: The finest offerings at the temple
adults.option_d: Earthly power and fame
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:19 says, "My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver."

adults.question: What does the theological concept of wisdom's pre-existence in Proverbs 8 foreshadow in Christian interpretation?
adults.option_a: The eternal Word of God, the Logos, identified as Christ in John 1
adults.option_b: The law given on Sinai
adults.option_c: The Spirit moving on the waters in Genesis 1
adults.option_d: The angel of the LORD appearing to Abraham
adults.explanation: The personification of wisdom in Proverbs 8 as present before creation is widely seen as prefiguring the eternal Logos — Christ — of John 1:1-3.

adults.question: What does Proverbs 8:32-33 instruct those who have heard wisdom's call?
adults.option_a: Hearken unto me, hear instruction and be wise, refuse it not
adults.option_b: Return to her daily as to a spring of water
adults.option_c: Teach others what wisdom has taught thee
adults.option_d: Seek wisdom even if the cost is great
adults.explanation: Proverbs 8:32-33 says, "Now therefore hearken unto me...Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not."

kids.question: How many pillars did Wisdom build in her house in Proverbs 9?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 9:1 says, "Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 9:10 say is the beginning of wisdom?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 9:10 says, "The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom."

kids.question: What does Folly do in Proverbs 9?
kids.option_a: She sits at the door of her house and calls to passers-by
kids.option_b: She roams the streets looking for the simple
kids.option_c: She stands at the city gate weeping
kids.explanation: Proverbs 9:14 says, "For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city."

teens.question: What did Wisdom do after building her house and preparing her feast in Proverbs 9?
teens.option_a: She sent out her maidens and called from the high places of the city
teens.option_b: She went personally to every home
teens.option_c: She sent letters to the simple
teens.option_d: She waited silently at her door
teens.explanation: Proverbs 9:3 says, "She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 9:7-8 say about correcting a scorner?
teens.option_a: He that reproveth a scorner getteth shame; reprove not a scorner lest he hate thee
teens.option_b: A scorner will eventually respond to patience
teens.option_c: A scorner should be avoided completely
teens.option_d: A scorner needs public rebuke
teens.explanation: Proverbs 9:7-8 says, "He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame...reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 9:9 promise if you give instruction to a wise man?
teens.option_a: He will be yet wiser; teach a just man and he will increase in learning
teens.option_b: He will teach it to others
teens.option_c: He will honour thee above all men
teens.option_d: He will return the favour doubly
teens.explanation: Proverbs 9:9 says, "Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning."

teens.question: What deceptive invitation does Folly make in Proverbs 9:17?
teens.option_a: Stolen waters are sweet and bread eaten in secret is pleasant
teens.option_b: Come in, for all is prepared for you
teens.option_c: The wise man is wrong; my way is better
teens.option_d: Leave wisdom and find true pleasure here
teens.explanation: Proverbs 9:17 says, "Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant."

teens.question: What does the guest of Folly not know in Proverbs 9:18?
teens.option_a: That the dead are there and her guests are in the depths of hell
teens.option_b: That she has stolen what she offers
teens.option_c: That her house is built on sand
teens.option_d: That the LORD watches their secret deeds
teens.explanation: Proverbs 9:18 says, "But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell."

adults.question: What does Wisdom's feast in Proverbs 9:2-5 include, and what does she invite the simple to do?
adults.option_a: She slew her beasts and mingled wine; she invites the simple to forsake foolishness and live
adults.option_b: She prepared a great banquet for the righteous only
adults.option_c: She offered bread and water to those who sought her
adults.option_d: She invited kings and nobles to her table
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:2-4 says she prepared meat, mingled wine and says, "Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine which I have mingled."

adults.question: What distinction does Proverbs 9:8 draw between a scorner and a wise man in how they receive rebuke?
adults.option_a: Rebuke a wise man and he will love thee, but a scorner hates the reprover
adults.option_b: The scorner argues while the wise man is silent
adults.option_c: The scorner flees while the wise man stays
adults.option_d: The scorner weeps while the wise man gives thanks
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:8 says, "Reprove not a scorner...rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee."

adults.question: How does Proverbs 9:10 define true knowledge as distinct from the beginning of wisdom?
adults.option_a: The knowledge of the holy is understanding
adults.option_b: Wisdom leads to knowledge which leads to truth
adults.option_c: Fear is wisdom, knowledge is its fruit
adults.option_d: The holy Scriptures are the source of all knowledge
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:10 says, "The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding."

adults.question: What personal benefit does Proverbs 9:12 assign to wisdom and scorn?
adults.option_a: If thou art wise, thou art wise for thyself; if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it
adults.option_b: Wisdom benefits your family; scorn harms your enemies
adults.option_c: Wisdom enriches all around you; scorn impoverishes the fool only
adults.option_d: Wisdom serves God; scorn serves no one
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:12 says, "If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it."

adults.question: What does Wisdom's complete invitation in Proverbs 9:6 call the simple to do?
adults.option_a: Forsake the foolish, and live; go in the way of understanding
adults.option_b: Come to the feast and eat freely
adults.option_c: Turn from all wickedness and follow her
adults.option_d: Seek the LORD with all your heart
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:6 says, "Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 9:11 say wisdom does for the years of the seeker?
adults.option_a: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased
adults.option_b: She adds honour to all the years of thy life
adults.option_c: She fills the years with understanding
adults.option_d: She gives thee years of peace and rest
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:11 says, "For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased."

adults.question: What structural contrast does Proverbs 9 present between Wisdom's house and Folly's house?
adults.option_a: Wisdom's house is ancient; Folly's house is new
adults.option_b: Wisdom's house is on a hill; Folly's house is in a valley
adults.option_c: Wisdom's house is open to all; Folly's house is hidden
adults.option_d: Wisdom builds on seven pillars, slays beasts and mingles wine; Folly sits idle at her door with stolen water and secret bread
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:1-5 shows Wisdom building, preparing, and sending out; Proverbs 9:13-18 shows Folly sitting, calling, and offering only "stolen waters."

adults.question: In Proverbs 9:13-16, who specifically does Folly target with her call?
adults.option_a: Whoso is simple and him that wanteth understanding
adults.option_b: The rich young ruler
adults.option_c: The wise man who has strayed
adults.option_d: The man who rejected Wisdom
adults.explanation: Proverbs 9:16 says, "Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: and as for him that wanteth understanding."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 10:1 say a wise son does for his father?
kids.option_d: He bringeth peace to his father
kids.explanation: Proverbs 10:1 says, "A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 10:12 say love does to sins?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 10:12 says, "love covereth all sins."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 10:7 say about the memory of the just?
kids.option_a: The memory of the just is blessed
kids.option_b: The memory of the just lasts forever
kids.option_c: The memory of the just is treasured by God
kids.option_d: The memory of the just comforts the living
kids.explanation: Proverbs 10:7 says, "The memory of the just is blessed."

teens.question: What contrast does Proverbs 10:4 draw about diligence and laziness?
teens.option_a: He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand, but the hand of the diligent maketh rich
teens.option_b: The lazy man sleeps while the diligent man prays
teens.option_c: Laziness brings shame while diligence brings honour
teens.option_d: The lazy man wastes time while the diligent man saves it
teens.explanation: Proverbs 10:4 says, "He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 10:9 say about the person who walks uprightly?
teens.option_a: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely
teens.option_b: His path is always plain before him
teens.option_c: The LORD guides his every step
teens.option_d: His enemies cannot touch him
teens.explanation: Proverbs 10:9 says, "He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 10:19 teach about many words?
teens.option_a: In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin; he that refraineth his lips is wise
teens.option_b: Many words prove a man's wisdom
teens.option_c: The wise man speaks freely among friends
teens.option_d: Words are measured by their quality not quantity
teens.explanation: Proverbs 10:19 says, "In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips is wise."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 10:22 say about the blessing of the LORD?
teens.option_a: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it
teens.option_b: The blessing of the LORD comes to those who seek him early
teens.option_c: The blessing of the LORD is wisdom above all things
teens.option_d: The blessing of the LORD is given freely to the humble
teens.explanation: Proverbs 10:22 says, "The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 10:25 say about the righteous when a whirlwind passes?
teens.option_a: The righteous is an everlasting foundation
teens.option_b: The righteous bends but does not break
teens.option_c: The righteous flees to safety
teens.option_d: The righteous trusts and is not moved
teens.explanation: Proverbs 10:25 says, "As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:2 say about treasures of wickedness?
adults.option_a: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death
adults.option_b: Wicked treasure fades quickly
adults.option_c: Stolen goods bring a curse
adults.option_d: The LORD takes away what wickedness gathers
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:2 says, "Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:11 say the mouth of the righteous is?
adults.option_a: A well of life; but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked
adults.option_b: A source of blessing to all who hear
adults.option_c: A lamp that guideth others in darkness
adults.option_d: A sword that cutteth down the wicked
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:11 says, "The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked."

adults.question: What literary form characterises most proverbs in chapters 10-15 and what is it called?
adults.option_a: Antithetical parallelism — two contrasting lines joined by "but"
adults.option_b: Synonymous parallelism — two lines saying the same thing
adults.option_c: Synthetic parallelism — two lines building on each other
adults.option_d: Chiastic structure — mirror-image parallelism
adults.explanation: Chapters 10-15 are dominated by antithetical proverbs where the second line contrasts the first with "but," e.g., Proverbs 10:1.

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:17 say about keeping instruction and refusing reproof?
adults.option_a: He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction; he that refuseth reproof erreth
adults.option_b: Keeping instruction brings wealth and refusing it brings poverty
adults.option_c: Instruction leads to wisdom; refusing it leads to foolishness
adults.option_d: The keeper of instruction is honoured; the refuser is forgotten
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:17 says, "He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:26 compare a lazy messenger to?
adults.option_a: Vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes
adults.option_b: A broken wheel on a cart
adults.option_c: A stone in a smooth path
adults.option_d: A cloud without rain
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:26 says, "As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:12 contrast with love covering sins?
adults.option_a: Hatred stirreth up strifes
adults.option_b: Envy destroys relationships
adults.option_c: Folly reveals all shame
adults.option_d: Pride multiplies offences
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:12 says, "Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:20 say the heart of the wicked is?
adults.option_a: Little worth
adults.option_b: Full of deceit
adults.option_c: Dark as night
adults.option_d: Empty as a broken vessel
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:20 says, "The tongue of the just is as choice silver: the heart of the wicked is little worth."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 10:31-32 say about the mouth of the righteous versus the froward?
adults.option_a: The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom; the froward tongue shall be cut out
adults.option_b: The righteous speak truth while the froward speak lies
adults.option_c: The just are heard by all while the froward are ignored
adults.option_d: The righteous speak seldom but well; the froward speak often but poorly
adults.explanation: Proverbs 10:31-32 says, "The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom...The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 11:2 say comes with pride?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 11:2 says, "When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 11:14 say a nation needs for safety?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 11:14 says, "but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 11:24 say about the generous person?
kids.option_a: There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth
kids.option_b: He who gives receives double in return
kids.option_c: The generous man is loved by all
kids.option_d: He who shares his bread will never go hungry
kids.explanation: Proverbs 11:24 says, "There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more than is meet, but it tendeth to poverty."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 11:1 say the LORD abhors?
teens.option_a: A false balance
teens.option_b: A proud look
teens.option_c: A lying tongue
teens.option_d: A wicked heart
teens.explanation: Proverbs 11:1 says, "A false balance is abomination to the LORD: but a just weight is his delight."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 11:13 say about a talebearer?
teens.option_a: A talebearer revealeth secrets; but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter
teens.option_b: A talebearer is hated by all men
teens.option_c: A talebearer brings division to families
teens.option_d: A talebearer shall be punished by the elders
teens.explanation: Proverbs 11:13 says, "A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 11:17 say about a merciful man?
teens.option_a: He doeth good to his own soul
teens.option_b: He is rewarded with long life
teens.option_c: He finds favour with God and men
teens.option_d: He is protected from his enemies
teens.explanation: Proverbs 11:17 says, "The merciful man doeth good to his own soul: but he that is cruel troubleth his own flesh."

teens.question: What image does Proverbs 11:22 use for a beautiful woman without discretion?
teens.option_a: A jewel of gold in a swine's snout
teens.option_b: A rose growing among thorns
teens.option_c: A star that shines by day
teens.option_d: A lamp left unguarded in the wind
teens.explanation: Proverbs 11:22 says, "As a jewel of gold in a swine's snout, so is a fair woman which is without discretion."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 11:30 compare the fruit of the righteous to?
teens.option_a: A tree of life
teens.option_b: Choice silver
teens.option_c: A flowing fountain
teens.option_d: The morning light
teens.explanation: Proverbs 11:30 says, "The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:3 say guides the upright versus what destroys the treacherous?
adults.option_a: The integrity of the upright shall guide them, the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them
adults.option_b: Wisdom guides the upright; folly destroys the wicked
adults.option_c: Truth guides the righteous; lies destroy the liar
adults.option_d: The law guides the upright; sin destroys the transgressor
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:3 says, "The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:4 declare about riches in the day of wrath?
adults.option_a: Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death
adults.option_b: Riches can buy a little time but not salvation
adults.option_c: The wicked man's riches are taken away suddenly
adults.option_d: A man's wealth cannot follow him beyond the grave
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:4 says, "Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:9 say the hypocrite does with his mouth?
adults.option_a: He destroyeth his neighbour
adults.option_b: He deceiveth those who trust him
adults.option_c: He spreadeth mischief through the city
adults.option_d: He bringeth shame upon his family
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:9 says, "An hypocrite with his mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the just be delivered."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:18 contrast about the wages of the wicked versus the righteous?
adults.option_a: The wicked worketh a deceitful work, but he that soweth righteousness hath a sure reward
adults.option_b: The wicked earn great wages now but lose all later
adults.option_c: The wicked gain in secret while the righteous gain openly
adults.option_d: The wicked's wages perish while the righteous's are stored up in heaven
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:18 says, "The wicked worketh a deceitful work: but to him that soweth righteousness shall be a sure reward."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:25 say about the liberal soul?
adults.option_a: The liberal soul shall be made fat; he that watereth shall be watered also himself
adults.option_b: The generous man shall receive from the LORD
adults.option_c: The open hand will never be empty
adults.option_d: He who gives to the poor lends to the LORD
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:25 says, "The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:26 say about the man who withholds grain?
adults.option_a: The people shall curse him
adults.option_b: He shall be known as a miser
adults.option_c: God shall judge his greed
adults.option_d: He loses his blessing from above
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:26 says, "He that withholdeth corn, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be upon the head of him that selleth it."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:29 say about the one who troubleth his own house?
adults.option_a: He shall inherit the wind
adults.option_b: He shall be cast out of the city
adults.option_c: He shall lose his inheritance
adults.option_d: He shall come to poverty and shame
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:29 says, "He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 11:31 establish about the recompense of the righteous and sinner on earth?
adults.option_a: Behold, the righteous shall be recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner
adults.option_b: God rewards the righteous in heaven and punishes the wicked on earth
adults.option_c: The earth is a place of testing for all
adults.option_d: Justice comes to all men before death
adults.explanation: Proverbs 11:31 says, "Behold, the righteous shall be recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 12:1 say about loving instruction?
kids.option_b: He who loves instruction will become wise
kids.option_c: He who loves learning is loved by God
kids.explanation: Proverbs 12:1 says, "Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof is brutish."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 12:10 say a righteous man does for animals?
kids.option_a: He regardeth the life of his animal
kids.option_b: He feeds his animal before himself
kids.option_c: He trains his animal with kindness
kids.option_d: He protects his animal from harm
kids.explanation: Proverbs 12:10 says, "A righteous man regardeth the life of his animal."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 12:17 say a true witness delivers?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 12:17 says, "An honest witness delivereth right things: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies." — "right" reflects truthful, upright testimony.

teens.question: What does Proverbs 12:4 say a virtuous woman is to her husband?
teens.option_a: A crown to her husband
teens.option_b: His strength and honour
teens.option_c: His joy and comfort
teens.option_d: His wisdom and guide
teens.explanation: Proverbs 12:4 says, "A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 12:11 say about one who follows vain persons?
teens.option_a: He is void of understanding
teens.option_b: He will come to poverty
teens.option_c: He will lose his friends
teens.option_d: He shall be ashamed among men
teens.explanation: Proverbs 12:11 says, "but he that followeth vain persons is void of understanding."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 12:15 say about a fool's view of himself?
teens.option_a: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes
teens.option_b: A fool believes he is wiser than all
teens.option_c: A fool never seeks instruction
teens.option_d: A fool despises the counsel of the wise
teens.explanation: Proverbs 12:15 says, "The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 12:18 contrast about rash words and the tongue of the wise?
teens.option_a: Speaking like piercings of a sword vs the tongue of the wise is health
teens.option_b: Harsh words cut deep while kind words comfort
teens.option_c: Rash words destroy while wise words build up
teens.option_d: Foolish speech spreads pain while wisdom speaks sparingly
teens.explanation: Proverbs 12:18 says, "There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise is health."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 12:24 say about the hand of the diligent?
teens.option_a: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule
teens.option_b: The diligent man shall not lack
teens.option_c: The diligent hand is blessed of God
teens.option_d: The diligent worker earns more than others
teens.explanation: Proverbs 12:24 says, "The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:2 say about a man of wicked devices?
adults.option_a: The LORD will condemn him
adults.option_b: He shall fall by his own schemes
adults.option_c: His wickedness shall be exposed
adults.option_d: He gains the hatred of all men
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:2 says, "A good man obtaineth favour of the LORD: but a man of wicked devices will he condemn."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:5 say the thoughts of the righteous are versus the counsels of the wicked?
adults.option_a: The thoughts of the righteous are right; the counsels of the wicked are deceit
adults.option_b: The righteous think deeply; the wicked act rashly
adults.option_c: The righteous are transparent; the wicked are hidden
adults.option_d: The righteous think of others; the wicked think of themselves
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:5 says, "The thoughts of the righteous are right: but the counsels of the wicked are deceit."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:7 say about the house of the righteous versus the wicked being overthrown?
adults.option_a: The wicked are overthrown and are not; but the house of the righteous shall stand
adults.option_b: The wicked fall quickly while the righteous rise slowly
adults.option_c: God pulls down the wicked's house but builds the righteous's
adults.option_d: The wicked build on sand; the righteous on rock
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:7 says, "The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:14 teach about the fruit of a man's mouth?
adults.option_a: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth
adults.option_b: A man's speech reveals what is in his heart
adults.option_c: A man's words return to him in kind
adults.option_d: A man's lips either bless or curse him
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:14 says, "A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:19 promise about truthful lips versus a lying tongue?
adults.option_a: Truthful lips shall be established for ever; a lying tongue is but for a moment
adults.option_b: Truth is rewarded and lies are punished
adults.option_c: The truth teller is trusted while the liar is shunned
adults.option_d: God preserves the truthful word and destroys the false
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:19 says, "The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue is but for a moment."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:20 say about the heart of those who imagine evil versus those who counsel peace?
adults.option_a: Deceit is in the heart of them that imagine evil; joy to the counsellors of peace
adults.option_b: Evil is hidden in the heart but joy is spoken aloud
adults.option_c: The schemer finds no rest but the peacemaker finds rest
adults.option_d: Deceit brings destruction and peace brings prosperity
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:20 says, "Deceit is in the heart of them that imagine evil: but to the counsellors of peace is joy."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:25 say lifts up the spirit of a heavy-hearted man?
adults.option_a: A good word maketh it glad
adults.option_b: The comfort of friends restores the spirit
adults.option_c: The LORD lifts the spirit of the sad
adults.option_d: Prayer turns heaviness into joy
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:25 says, "Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 12:26 say the righteous is to his neighbour?
adults.option_a: More excellent than his neighbour
adults.option_b: A guide to the righteous path
adults.option_c: A source of wisdom and strength
adults.option_d: A shield in times of trouble
adults.explanation: Proverbs 12:26 says, "The righteous is more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 13:20 say happens to those who walk with wise men?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 13:20 says, "He that walketh with wise men shall be wise."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 13:24 say a parent who spares the rod does?
kids.option_b: He honoureth his son above God
kids.explanation: Proverbs 13:24 says, "He that spareth his rod hateth his son."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 13:3 say about guarding the mouth?
kids.option_a: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life
kids.option_b: A guarded mouth speaks only truth
kids.option_c: The careful tongue avoids all sin
kids.option_d: A wise child is slow to speak
kids.explanation: Proverbs 13:3 says, "He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 13:1 contrast about a wise son and a scorner?
teens.option_a: A wise son heareth his father's instruction but a scorner heareth not rebuke
teens.option_b: A wise son prospers but a scorner fails
teens.option_c: A wise son is loved but a scorner is despised
teens.option_d: A wise son speaks carefully but a scorner speaks rashly
teens.explanation: Proverbs 13:1 says, "A wise son heareth his father's instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 13:7 observe about wealth and poverty?
teens.option_a: There is that maketh himself rich, yet hath nothing; and that maketh himself poor, yet hath great riches
teens.option_b: Riches deceive while poverty reveals character
teens.option_c: The poor man may be richer in God's sight
teens.option_d: Wealth is not always what it appears to be
teens.explanation: Proverbs 13:7 says, "There is that maketh himself rich, yet hath nothing: and there is that maketh himself poor, yet hath great riches."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 13:11 say about wealth gotten by vanity?
teens.option_a: It shall be diminished; but he that gathereth by labour shall increase
teens.option_b: It brings no lasting satisfaction
teens.option_c: It tempts a man into further sin
teens.option_d: It brings judgment upon the house
teens.explanation: Proverbs 13:11 says, "Wealth gotten by vanity shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour shall increase."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 13:12 say about hope deferred?
teens.option_a: Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life
teens.option_b: Deferred hope is better than no hope at all
teens.option_c: Waiting for hope tests the strength of the soul
teens.option_d: A man without hope is lost
teens.explanation: Proverbs 13:12 says, "Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life."
