teens.question: What does Proverbs 13:18 say about refusing instruction?
teens.option_a: Poverty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction
teens.option_b: He who rejects reproof will fall suddenly
teens.option_c: A man without instruction makes poor decisions
teens.option_d: Refusing instruction is the first step to ruin
teens.explanation: Proverbs 13:18 says, "Poverty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction: but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:4 say about the sluggard versus the diligent?
adults.option_a: The soul of the sluggard desireth and hath nothing; the soul of the diligent shall be made fat
adults.option_b: The lazy man wishes but the diligent man works
adults.option_c: The sluggard dreams while the diligent man acts
adults.option_d: Desiring without working leads to emptiness
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:4 says, "The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:9 contrast about the light of the righteous and the lamp of the wicked?
adults.option_a: The light of the righteous rejoiceth; but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out
adults.option_b: The righteous's light grows brighter while the wicked's dims
adults.option_c: The righteous shine in darkness; the wicked cannot find their way
adults.option_d: God tends the righteous's lamp but not the wicked's
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:9 says, "The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:10 say is the only source of contention?
adults.option_a: Only by pride cometh contention
adults.option_b: A quick temper is the root of all strife
adults.option_c: Envy and jealousy breed contention
adults.option_d: Folly creates strife among men
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:10 says, "Only by pride cometh contention: but with the well advised is wisdom."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:13 say about despising the word?
adults.option_a: Whoso despiseth the word shall be destroyed
adults.option_b: He who ignores wisdom faces its consequences
adults.option_c: The despiser of the word walks in darkness
adults.option_d: Rejecting God's word leads to spiritual death
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:13 says, "Whoso despiseth the word shall be destroyed: but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:15 say about the way of transgressors?
adults.option_a: The way of transgressors is hard
adults.option_b: Sinners walk a path of thorns
adults.option_c: The wicked road leads nowhere good
adults.option_d: Transgression makes every journey difficult
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:15 says, "Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors is hard."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:17 say about a wicked versus a faithful messenger?
adults.option_a: A wicked messenger falleth into mischief; but a faithful ambassador is health
adults.option_b: A wicked messenger brings bad tidings; a faithful one brings good news
adults.option_c: A lying messenger is punished; a truthful one is rewarded
adults.option_d: A lazy messenger fails his master; a diligent one succeeds
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:17 says, "A wicked messenger falleth into mischief: but a faithful ambassador is health."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:22 say about the inheritance of a good man versus the sinner's wealth?
adults.option_a: A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children's children; the sinner's wealth is laid up for the just
adults.option_b: A righteous man builds wealth that lasts three generations
adults.option_c: A good man stores up a spiritual inheritance
adults.option_d: The wicked's wealth is cursed and turns to nothing
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:22 says, "A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children's children: and the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the just."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 13:25 say about the difference in eating between the righteous and the wicked?
adults.option_a: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul; the belly of the wicked shall want
adults.option_b: The righteous eat little but are satisfied; the wicked eat much and are never full
adults.option_c: God provides for the righteous but withholds from the wicked
adults.option_d: The righteous's bread is blessed while the wicked's is cursed
adults.explanation: Proverbs 13:25 says, "The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul: but the belly of the wicked shall want."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 14:34 say righteousness does for a nation?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 14:34 says, "Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 14:12 say about a way that seems right to a person?
kids.option_a: The end thereof are the ways of death
kids.option_c: It may not be the best way
kids.option_d: It still needs to be tested
kids.explanation: Proverbs 14:12 says, "There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death."

kids.question: Who does Proverbs 14:21 say is happy?
kids.option_a: He that hath mercy on the poor
kids.explanation: Proverbs 14:21 says, "but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 14:1 contrast about a wise woman and a foolish one?
teens.option_a: Every wise woman buildeth her house; but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands
teens.option_b: A wise woman speaks carefully but a foolish one hastily
teens.option_c: A wise woman is patient but a foolish one is rash
teens.option_d: A wise woman saves but a foolish one wastes
teens.explanation: Proverbs 14:1 says, "Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 14:10 say about the bitterness of the heart?
teens.option_a: The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy
teens.option_b: The heart that is bitter cannot receive joy
teens.option_c: Deep pain is known only to God and oneself
teens.option_d: A bitter heart cannot find peace
teens.explanation: Proverbs 14:10 says, "The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 14:13 say about laughter?
teens.option_a: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness
teens.option_b: Laughter is the medicine of the soul
teens.option_c: A laughing man hides his true feelings
teens.option_d: Joy without truth is empty and fleeting
teens.explanation: Proverbs 14:13 says, "Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 14:17 say about a quick-tempered man?
teens.option_a: He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly
teens.option_b: A hot-tempered man invites trouble
teens.option_c: Anger without cause is a sin
teens.option_d: A quick temper reveals a foolish heart
teens.explanation: Proverbs 14:17 says, "He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked devices is hated."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 14:30 contrast about envy and a sound heart?
teens.option_a: A sound heart is the life of the flesh; but envy the rottenness of the bones
teens.option_b: A sound heart brings peace; envy brings strife
teens.option_c: Contentment gives life; envy destroys it
teens.option_d: A peaceful heart is a blessing; envy is a curse
teens.explanation: Proverbs 14:30 says, "A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:4 say about where no oxen are?
adults.option_a: The crib is clean: but much increase is by the strength of the ox
adults.option_b: There is no ploughing and no harvest
adults.option_c: The barn is empty and the farmer is poor
adults.option_d: The field lies fallow and the work is not done
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:4 says, "Where no oxen are, the crib is clean: but much increase is by the strength of the ox."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:6 say about the scorner and wisdom?
adults.option_a: A scorner seeketh wisdom and findeth it not; but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth
adults.option_b: The scorner cannot receive what he mocks
adults.option_c: Wisdom is hidden from the proud heart
adults.option_d: He who despises truth will never find it
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:6 says, "A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:20 reveal about social attitudes toward the poor and the rich?
adults.option_a: The poor is hated even of his own neighbour; but the rich hath many friends
adults.option_b: Society judges a man by his wealth
adults.option_c: Poverty brings rejection while wealth brings false friends
adults.option_d: Men court the rich but abandon the poor
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:20 says, "The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich hath many friends."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:23 say about talk without labour?
adults.option_a: In all labour there is profit; but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury
adults.option_b: Empty speech wastes the day
adults.option_c: A man who only talks will come to nothing
adults.option_d: Speaking without acting is the way of the sluggard
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:23 says, "In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:26-27 say about the fear of the LORD?
adults.option_a: In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence; it is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death
adults.option_b: The fear of the LORD gives strength in old age
adults.option_c: Those who fear the LORD will never be put to shame
adults.option_d: The fear of the LORD is the foundation of all virtue
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:26-27 says, "In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence...The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:29 contrast about a slow and hasty spirit?
adults.option_a: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding; but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly
adults.option_b: Patience is strength; haste is weakness
adults.option_c: The slow temper earns respect; the hot temper earns shame
adults.option_d: Anger controlled is wisdom; anger uncontrolled is foolishness
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:29 says, "He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:31 say about oppressing the poor?
adults.option_a: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker
adults.option_b: Oppressing the poor is an abomination to God
adults.option_c: The oppressor of the poor will answer to God
adults.option_d: He who harms the poor sins against heaven
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:31 says, "He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 14:35 say about the king and his servants?
adults.option_a: The king's favour is toward a wise servant: but his wrath is against him that causeth shame
adults.option_b: A king rewards those who bring him good news
adults.option_c: The king judges all men by their words
adults.option_d: A servant who pleases the king gains great honour
adults.explanation: Proverbs 14:35 says, "The king's favour is toward a wise servant: but his wrath is against him that causeth shame."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 15:1 say a soft answer turns away?
kids.explanation: Proverbs 15:1 says, "A soft answer turneth away wrath."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 15:3 say about the eyes of the LORD?
kids.option_a: They are in every place, beholding the evil and the good
kids.option_c: They watch over the righteous always
kids.option_d: They never close and never sleep
kids.explanation: Proverbs 15:3 says, "The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good."

kids.question: What does Proverbs 15:17 say is better than a stalled ox with hatred?
kids.option_a: A dinner of herbs where love is
kids.option_b: A small meal shared with friends
kids.explanation: Proverbs 15:17 says, "Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 15:1 contrast with a soft answer?
teens.option_a: A grievous word stirreth up anger
teens.option_b: A harsh tone creates conflict
teens.option_c: A loud voice increases anger
teens.option_d: A proud reply makes things worse
teens.explanation: Proverbs 15:1 says, "A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 15:8 say about the prayer of the upright?
teens.option_a: It is his delight
teens.option_b: It is heard immediately
teens.option_c: It rises before God
teens.option_d: It is answered in full
teens.explanation: Proverbs 15:8 says, "but the prayer of the upright is his delight."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 15:13 say a merry heart does?
teens.option_a: It maketh a cheerful countenance
teens.option_b: It bringeth joy to others
teens.option_c: It lifts up the spirit
teens.option_d: It is a gift from God
teens.explanation: Proverbs 15:13 says, "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 15:22 say about plans made without counsel?
teens.option_a: Without counsel purposes are disappointed
teens.option_b: Plans made alone often fail
teens.option_c: The man who consults no one is a fool
teens.option_d: A plan without prayer is doomed to fail
teens.explanation: Proverbs 15:22 says, "Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established."

teens.question: What does Proverbs 15:29 say about the LORD and the wicked versus the righteous?
teens.option_a: The LORD is far from the wicked; but he heareth the prayer of the righteous
teens.option_b: The LORD judges the wicked but saves the righteous
teens.option_c: The LORD sees the wicked and punishes them
teens.option_d: The LORD turns away from the wicked but draws near the righteous
teens.explanation: Proverbs 15:29 says, "The LORD is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:4 contrast about a wholesome tongue and a perverse one?
adults.option_a: A wholesome tongue is a tree of life; but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit
adults.option_b: A wholesome tongue heals; a perverse one harms
adults.option_c: Kind words build up; cruel words tear down
adults.option_d: A good tongue brings blessing; a wicked one brings a curse
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:4 says, "A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:11 say about Hell and Destruction before the LORD?
adults.option_a: Hell and destruction are before the LORD; how much more then the hearts of the children of men
adults.option_b: Even the depths of hell are visible to God
adults.option_c: No secret place is hidden from the LORD
adults.option_d: God sees beyond the grave into eternity
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:11 says, "Hell and destruction are before the LORD: how much more then the hearts of the children of men?"

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:14 say the heart of the discerning seeks versus what the fool feeds on?
adults.option_a: The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge; the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness
adults.option_b: The wise man hungers for wisdom; the fool is content with nothing
adults.option_c: An understanding heart digs deep; a foolish heart stays shallow
adults.option_d: The discerning seek to learn; the foolish seek only to speak
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:14 says, "The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:15 say about the cheerful heart versus the afflicted?
adults.option_a: All the days of the afflicted are evil; but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast
adults.option_b: The sad man sees only trouble; the happy man sees only good
adults.option_c: Affliction clouds every day; joy brightens every one
adults.option_d: The sorrowful live in shadow while the joyful live in light
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:15 says, "All the days of the afflicted are evil: but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:16-17 say is better than great treasure and a stalled ox?
adults.option_a: Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble; better a dinner of herbs where love is
adults.option_b: A simple life of godliness exceeds wealth filled with anxiety
adults.option_c: Peace with God is worth more than earthly prosperity
adults.option_d: Contentment surpasses abundance without righteousness
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:16-17 says, "Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith. Better is a dinner of herbs where love is."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:23 say about an apt word spoken in due season?
adults.option_a: A word spoken in due season, how good is it
adults.option_b: A timely word has power to heal a broken heart
adults.option_c: Speaking at the right time brings the greatest result
adults.option_d: A well-timed word is a rare and precious gift
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:23 says, "A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth: and a word spoken in due season, how good is it!"

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:25 say about the proud man's house versus the widow?
adults.option_a: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud; but he will establish the border of the widow
adults.option_b: Pride brings a family to ruin while humility sustains it
adults.option_c: God tears down arrogance and builds up the helpless
adults.option_d: The proud man's house falls; the humble man's house stands
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:25 says, "The LORD will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow."

adults.question: What does Proverbs 15:33 say wisdom's instruction is and what must come before honour?
adults.option_a: The instruction of wisdom is the fear of the LORD; and before honour is humility
adults.option_b: Wisdom leads to honour and honour leads to lasting reputation
adults.option_c: Fear of God teaches all true wisdom and produces genuine honour
adults.option_d: The path to honour is paved with wisdom's hard lessons
adults.explanation: Proverbs 15:33 says, "The fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility."
